title: Keibul Lamjao National Park
tagline: Keibul Lamjao National Park — wildlife reserve of Manipur

overview.short: The Keibul Lamjao National Park (Meitei: Keibul Lamjao Leipakki Lampak) is a national park in the Bishnupur district of the state of Manipur in Northeast India.
overview.description: The Keibul Lamjao National Park (Meitei: Keibul Lamjao Leipakki Lampak) is a national park in the Bishnupur district of the state of Manipur in Northeast India. It is 40 km2 (15.4 mi2) in area, the only floating national park in the world, and an integral part of Loktak Lake. It is currently on the tentative lists of the UNESCO World Heritage Sites, under the title "Keibul Lamjao Conservation Area (KLCA)", additionally covering the buffer of Loktak Lake (140 sq km) and Pumlen Pat (43 sq. km).

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Loktak Folklore Museum
topPlaces.description: The Loktak Folklore Museum (Meitei: Loktak Khunnung Pukei Lankei Shanglen) or the Thanga Folklore Museum (Meitei: Thanga Khunnung Pukei Lankei Shanglen) is a folk museum in Thanga Island in the Loktak lake of Manipur, India. It cares for and displays a collection of artistic, cultural and historical artefacts associated with the Loktak lake.

topPlaces.name: Khuga River
topPlaces.description: Khuga River, also known as the Tuitha River, is a river in Manipur, India. It originates in the Churachandpur district and flows through the district for much of its course.

topPlaces.name: Moirang
topPlaces.description: Moirang (Meitei: /moi.rāŋ/) is a town in the Indian state of Manipur, best known for the tentatively listed UNESCO World Heritage Sites of the Keibul Lamjao Conservation Area (KLCA), covering Keibul Lamjao National Park (KLNP), the world's only floating national park, the buffer of Loktak Lake (140 sq km) and Pumlen Pat (43 sq. km).

topPlaces.name: Phumdi
topPlaces.description: Phumdi (Meitei pronunciation: /pʰúm.dí/), also known as Phumthi (Meitei pronunciation: /pʰúm.tʰí/) or simply Phum (Meitei pronunciation: /pʰúm/), are a series of floating islands in the Loktak Lake, located in Manipur state, in northeastern India. They cover a substantial part of the lake area and are heterogeneous masses of vegetation, soil and organic matter, in different stages of decay.

topPlaces.name: Kumbi
topPlaces.description: Kumbi is a town and a Municipality in Bishnupur District in the Indian state of Manipur. The Kumbi Constituency of Manipur Legislative Assembly was named after the village.

topPlaces.name: Pumlenpat
topPlaces.description: Pumlenpat is the second largest lake in Manipur after the Loktak Lake, situated about 68 kilometres (42 mi) south of Imphal (the capital of Manipur, a state in North East India) and about 45 kilometres (28 mi) from Thoubal. Just as Loktak Lake, the people situated around this lake depend on fishery products for their livelihood.

topPlaces.name: Hiyanglam
topPlaces.description: Hiyanglam is a village situated within Kakching district, Manipur, India. It has a population of about 8,872 people in 2011.

topPlaces.name: Loktak Lake
topPlaces.description: Loktak Lake (Meitei: Loktak Pat) is the largest freshwater lake in Northeast India. It is a pulsating lake, with a surface area varying from 250 km2 to 500 km2 during the rainy season with a typical area of 287 km2.

items.note: The Loktak Folklore Museum (Meitei: Loktak Khunnung Pukei Lankei Shanglen) or the Thanga Folklore Museum (Meitei: Thanga Khunnung Pukei Lankei Shanglen) is a folk museum in Thanga Island in the Loktak lake of Manipur, India. It cares for and displays a collection of artistic, cultural and historical artefacts associated with the Loktak lake.
Khuga River, also known as the Tuitha River, is a river in Manipur, India. It originates in the Churachandpur district and flows through the district for much of its course.

Moirang (Meitei: /moi.rāŋ/) is a town in the Indian state of Manipur, best known for the tentatively listed UNESCO World Heritage Sites of the Keibul Lamjao Conservation Area (KLCA), covering Keibul Lamjao National Park (KLNP), the world's only floating national park, the buffer of Loktak Lake (140 sq km) and Pumlen Pat (43 sq. km).

Phumdi (Meitei pronunciation: /pʰúm.dí/), also known as Phumthi (Meitei pronunciation: /pʰúm.tʰí/) or simply Phum (Meitei pronunciation: /pʰúm/), are a series of floating islands in the Loktak Lake, located in Manipur state, in northeastern India. They cover a substantial part of the lake area and are heterogeneous masses of vegetation, soil and organic matter, in different stages of decay.
Kumbi is a town and a Municipality in Bishnupur District in the Indian state of Manipur. The Kumbi Constituency of Manipur Legislative Assembly was named after the village.

Pumlenpat is the second largest lake in Manipur after the Loktak Lake, situated about 68 kilometres (42 mi) south of Imphal (the capital of Manipur, a state in North East India) and about 45 kilometres (28 mi) from Thoubal. Just as Loktak Lake, the people situated around this lake depend on fishery products for their livelihood.
Hiyanglam is a village situated within Kakching district, Manipur, India. It has a population of about 8,872 people in 2011.

Loktak Lake (Meitei: Loktak Pat) is the largest freshwater lake in Northeast India. It is a pulsating lake, with a surface area varying from 250 km2 to 500 km2 during the rainy season with a typical area of 287 km2.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Keibul Lamjao National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Keibul Lamjao National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Keibul Lamjao National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Imphal Airport (31 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (159 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Keibul Lamjao National Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Keibul Lamjao National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Keibul Lamjao National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Keibul Lamjao National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Keibul Lamjao National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Loktak Folklore Museum, Khuga River, Moirang, Phumdi, Kumbi.
